verdict_reasoning: VEGFA is a secreted heparin-binding growth factor in the PDGF/VEGF family. It is synthesized with a signal peptide, processed through the ER/Golgi, and secreted into the extracellular space where it signals to receptor tyrosine kinases (VEGFR1/2) on endothelial cells. While VEGFA binds heparan sulfate proteoglycans in the extracellular matrix and can form high-affinity gradients, it is not membrane-anchored at any stage of its lifecycle — no TM domain, no GPI anchor, no stable covalent tethering to a cell-surface partner that would wash-resist. The protein exists in equilibrium between the soluble pool and reversible interactions with ECM components. Heparin-binding properties allow gradient formation but do not constitute a stable surface anchor. No documented evidence of TM-precursor cleavage, cell-state-induced ectopic surface display, or non-covalent wash-resistant partner tethering.
